_comment: COMPRESSION finish line. Consumed by proposed `fulcrum scoreboard diff --dominance dominance-spec-comp.json <artifact.json>`. Compression has TWO axes (wall + exact bytes); the verdict is 2-D Pareto (see COMPRESSION-CAMPAIGN-DESIGN.md sec1). Two finish tiers: HULL-MEMBER (mandatory floor: gzippy not strictly dominated by any rival) and BAND-WIN (aspirational per level-class). pass_condition BLOCKS on the HULL floor and REPORTS on BAND-WIN so the mandatory bar gates CI while the stretch bar is tracked, not gated.
subject: gzippy-native

grid._expand: hull cells = for each box: box.threads x corpora x level_classes with require=HULL-MEMBER. bandwin cells = same coordinates with require=BAND-WIN. D6: a class is N/A ONLY when NO tool can meet the target on that corpus; if gzippy CAN meet a target but its measured points don't, the cell FAILS (not skip). gzippy always has a C3 point (L11==zopfli). Rival absence (e.g. igzip absent on m1) shrinks the field, it does not skip a gzippy claim.

boxes.field_note: arm64: gzippy C0=libdeflate (no ISA-L); igzip likely absent from field.
x86: gzippy C0=ISA-L; full field incl igzip, bgzip.
x86: gzippy C0=ISA-L; full field incl igzip, bgzip.

review_dispositions_applied: D1 INDETERMINATE verdict (WALL-VOID never collapses to MATCH); D2 WALL-TIE=TOST-equivalent only; D3 FRONTIER semantics (drop strictly-dominated rival points before aggregating; a dominated rival cannot force CONTESTED); D4 nested QUALITY-TARGET classes (r<=r(anchor)); D6 no-gzippy-candidate=FAIL not skip; D7 global hull universe; D8 metadata canonicalized, framing bytes counted.

verdict_evaluation.admissible_frontier: D3: field frontier = union of all tools' in-scope (wall,bytes) points with strictly-dominated points removed. gzippy is compared only against NON-DOMINATED rival points.
verdict_evaluation.HULL-MEMBER: PASS iff gzippy's point is NOT strictly dominated by any point on the GLOBAL field frontier (all tools, all levels, corpus-wide). never LOSS. wall-cert = FASTER/SLOWER/WALL-TIE(TOST)/WALL-VOID; WALL-VOID -> INDETERMINATE (re-measure), not PASS.
verdict_evaluation.BAND-WIN: D4: PASS iff among ALL (tool,level) points meeting the class target r<=r(anchor), a gzippy point is Pareto-best (fastest at-or-below target, no admissible rival simultaneously smaller AND faster).

pass_condition.block_on: all HULL-MEMBER cells PASS (INDETERMINATE blocks -> re-measure, does not pass)
pass_condition.on_gzippy_absent_but_target_achievable: FAIL (D6: INELIGIBLE is a failed claim, not a skip)

notes: HULL floor = gzippy is never a strictly-worse choice than any rival (never both slower AND larger). This is the achievable 2-D finish line.
BAND-WIN stretch = at gzippy's ratio it is the fastest AND nothing is simultaneously smaller+faster. VoI-ordered, never dropped (infinite-funding rule).

The BYTES axis is EXACT (integer output_bytes); only the WALL axis carries A/A+TOST significance. A 0.1%-larger output is a byte LOSS regardless of a wall tie -> that is what turns a naive speed-win into a CONTESTED cell.
C3_ZOPFLI additionally asserts gzippy L11 output sha256 == C-zopfli output (bit-identity, zopfli.rs:1-14) as a deterministic pass/fail on the bytes axis.
